revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Our Lady Beneficial Sky]][[en:draft:Our Lady Beneficial Sky]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Our Lady Beneficial Sky|Our Lady Beneficial Sky]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} [[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]][[Category:goodbye $1 1]][[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky-->Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple}}
==Overview==
'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва}} from {{ill|Lithuania|ru|Великое княжество Литовское}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”
== History and Venerated Copies == In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)}}.
The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.
The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых}}.
Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня}} in Moscow.
Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.
== See Also == * {{ill|Inexhaustible Chalice|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет}} * {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов}}
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=306-307}} * {{Православная энциклопедия|149309|Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God|5|301-302|{{ill|Shchennikova, Lyudmila Alexandrovna|ru|Щенникова, Людмила Александровна}}}}

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Our Lady Beneficial Sky]][[en:draft:Our Lady Beneficial Sky]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Our Lady Beneficial Sky|Our Lady Beneficial Sky]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} [[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]][[Category:goodbye $1 1]][[Category: Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: categories]] <!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky-->Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple}}
==Overview==
'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва}} from {{ill|Lithuania|ru|Великое княжество Литовское}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”
== History and Venerated Copies == In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)}}.
The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.
The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых}}.
Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня}} in Moscow.
Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from [[:en:Lithuania|Lithuania]] by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

[[ru:Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо»]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]]

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from [[:en:Lithuania|Lithuania]] by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from [[:en:Lithuania|Lithuania]] by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from [[:en:Lithuania|Lithuania]] by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]]

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from [[:en:Lithuania|Lithuania]] by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from [[:en:Lithuania|Lithuania]] by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from [[:en:Lithuania|Lithuania]] by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from [[:en:Lithuania|Lithuania]] by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}}

{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to {{ill|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|en|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|WD=Q13429672|lt=Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion}} to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Our Lady Beneficial Sky]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Our Lady Beneficial Sky|Our Lady Beneficial Sky]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from {{ill|Lithuania|en|Lithuania|WD=Q37|lt=Lithuania}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inexhaustible Chalice|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inexhaustible Chalice|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=}}

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from {{ill|Lithuania|en|Lithuania|WD=Q37|lt=Lithuania}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]]

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}
==Overview==
'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from {{ill|Lithuania|en|Lithuania|WD=Q37|lt=Lithuania}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”
== History and Venerated Copies == In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.
The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.
The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.
Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral. == See Also == * {{ill|Inexhaustible Chalice|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inexhaustible Chalice|qq=}} * {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=}}
== References == {{примечания}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from {{ill|Lithuania|en|Lithuania|WD=Q37|lt=Lithuania}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from {{ill|Lithuania|en|Lithuania|WD=Q37|lt=Lithuania}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inexhaustible Chalice|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inexhaustible Chalice|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]]
[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}
==Overview==
'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from {{ill|Lithuania|en|Lithuania|WD=Q37|lt=Lithuania|1=Lithuania}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”
== History and Venerated Copies == In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.
The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.
The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.
Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.
Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral. == See Also == * {{ill|Inexhaustible Chalice|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inexhaustible Chalice|qq=}} * {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=}}
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=306-307}} * {{Православная энциклопедия|149309|Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God|5|301-302|{{ill|Shchennikova, Lyudmila Alexandrovna|ru|Щенникова, Людмила Александровна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shchennikova, Lyudmila Alexandrovna|qq=}}}}
==See Also== * {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|lt=Conqueror of Breads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}} * {{ill|Unfading Flower|lt=Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый_Цвет|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}} * {{ill|Madonna of the Carnation|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna of the Carnation|qq=}}* * {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References== {{reflist}}

[[ru:%D0%91%D0%BB%D0%B0%D0%B3%D0%BE%D0%B4%D0%B0%D1%82%D0%BD%D0%BE%D0%B5 %D0%9D%D0%B5%D0%B1%D0%BE]]
<!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--><!--Our Lady Beneficial Sky--> <!-- no translated page template found in history --> [[en:Our Lady Beneficial Sky]] {{wikidata link|Q4087860}} [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Marian apparitions]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]]


 

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from {{ill|Lithuania|en|Lithuania|WD=Q37|lt=Lithuania|1=Lithuania}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from {{ill|Lithuania|en|Lithuania|WD=Q37|lt=Lithuania|1=Lithuania}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Blagodatnoe Nebo]][[pl:Ikona Matki Bożej „Błogosławione Niebo”]][[ru:Благодатное Небо]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Благодатне Небо»]][[uz:Muborak Osmon]]


 

[[File:Икона_Благодатное_небо.jpg|thumb]]Blessed Sky is an icon of the Virgin mary, a {{ill|Marian apparition|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}

==Overview==

'''Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Пресвятой Богородицы «Благодатное Небо») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, traditionally brought to {{ill|Moscow|ru|Москва|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moscow|qq=}} from {{ill|Lithuania|en|Lithuania|WD=Q37|lt=Lithuania|1=Lithuania}} by {{ill|Sophia Vitovtovna|ru|Софья Витовтовна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophia Vitovtovna|qq=}}, the wife of Grand Prince {{ill|Vasily I of Moscow|ru|Василий I Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily I of Moscow|qq=}}. The icon was previously kept in {{ill|Smolensk|ru|Смоленск|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Smolensk|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Singayevsky V.|ru|Сингаевский В.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Singayevsky V.|qq=}} *Famous Russian Icons*. — M.: Astrel, 2010 — ISBN 978-5-271-26497-9 — p. 69</ref>. The name derives from the Marian hymn of the First Hour: “What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you have shone forth the Sun of Righteousness.”

== History and Venerated Copies ==
In 1678–1680, the {{ill|Armory Chamber|ru|Оружейная палата|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armory Chamber|qq=}} iconographers created a copy for the {{ill|Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|ru|Архангельский собор (Москва)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archangel Cathedral (Moscow Kremlin)|qq=}}. In 1682, master {{ill|Vasily Poznansky|ru|Познанский, Василий|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vasily Poznansky|qq=}} produced an applique version for the {{ill|Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|ru|Крестовоздвиженская церковь (Московский Кремль)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Exaltation of the Cross (Terem Palace)|qq=}}.

The icon depicts the {{ill|Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|ru|Жена, облечённая в солнце|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vision of the Woman Clothed with the Sun|qq=}} from the Revelation of {{ill|John the Theologian|ru|Иоанн Богослов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John the Theologian|qq=}}. The Mother of God stands full-length with the Christ Child on her left arm, surrounded by a solar {{ill|mandorla|ru|мандорла|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mandorla|qq=}}, with a crescent moon under her feet. Crowns adorn their heads.

The feast day is {{СС|19|March||6}} and also celebrated on the {{ill|Sunday of All Saints|ru|Неделя всех святых|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunday of All Saints|qq=}}.

Local venerated copies are kept in the {{ill|Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|ru|Храм Троицы Живоначальной на Воробьёвых горах|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Church of the Trinity on Vorobyovy Hills|qq=}} and the {{ill|Menshikov Tower|ru|Меншикова башня|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menshikov Tower|qq=}} in Moscow.

Believers pray to the Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon for healing from illnesses and for guidance of those living unrighteous lives toward the Christian faith. Some refer to the icon by the hymn’s phrase “What shall we call you.” The icon holds special veneration in Moscow, long housed in the Archangel Cathedral.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inexhaustible Chalice|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inexhaustible Chalice|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=}}

== References ==
{{примечания}}

== Literature ==
* {{книга|автор={{ill|Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|ru|Тальберг, Николай Дмитриевич|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Talberg, Nikolay Dmitrievich|qq=}}|заглавие=Detailed Menologion of Saints in the Russian Land|издание=Reprint|место=SPb.|издательство=Vera|год=1997|страницы=306-307}}
* {{Православная энциклопедия|149309|Blagodatnoye Nebo Icon of the Mother of God|5|301-302|{{ill|Shchennikova, Lyudmila Alexandrovna|ru|Щенникова, Людмила Александровна|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shchennikova, Lyudmila Alexandrovna|qq=}}}}

* {{ill|Madonna of the Carnation|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna of the Carnation|qq=}}*
* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}}* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}}* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}}* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}}* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}}* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}}==References==
